originContent: # Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis (TESSERA) [CVPR2026]
translatedContent: # Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis (TESSERA) [CVPR2026]

originContent: Satellite remote sensing enables a wide range of downstream applications, including habitat mapping, carbon accounting, and strategies for conservation and sustainable land use. However, satellite time series are voluminous and often cloud-corrupted, making them challenging to use: the scientific community's ability to extract actionable insights is often constrained by the scarcity of labelled training datasets and the computational burden of processing temporal data. The key insight behind our work, due to [Dr. Clement Atzberger](https://www.linkedin.com/in/clement-atzberger-8abb8065/) is that forcing auto-encoder embeddings derived from two cloud-free random samples of satellite time series to align using [Barlow Twins](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v139/zbontar21a/zbontar21a.pdf) results in an embedding that represents the entire time series, including the missing observations.
translatedContent: Satellite remote sensing enables a wide range of downstream applications, including habitat mapping, carbon accounting, and strategies for conservation and sustainable land use. However, satellite time series are voluminous and often cloud-corrupted, making them challenging to use: the scientific community's ability to extract actionable insights is often constrained by the scarcity of labelled training datasets and the computational burden of processing temporal data. The key insight behind our work, due to [Dr. Clement Atzberger](https://www.linkedin.com/in/clement-atzberger-8abb8065/) is that forcing auto-encoder embeddings derived from two cloud-free random samples of satellite time series to align using [Barlow Twins](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v139/zbontar21a/zbontar21a.pdf) results in an embedding that represents the entire time series, including the missing observations.

originContent: This idea is the key behind TESSERA, an open foundation model that preserves per-pixel spectral-temporal signals in 128-dimensional latent representations at 10-meter resolution globally. It uses self-supervised learning to summarise petabytes of Earth observation data. We compare our work with state-of-the-art task-specific models and other foundation models in five diverse downstream tasks and find that TESSERA closely matches or outperforms these baselines. By preserving temporal phenological signals that are typically lost in conventional approaches, TESSERA enables new insights into ecosystem dynamics, agricultural food systems, and environmental change detection. Moreover, our open-source implementation supports reproducibility and extensibility, while the privacy-preserving design allows researchers to maintain data sovereignty.
translatedContent: This idea is the key behind TESSERA, an open foundation model that preserves per-pixel spectral-temporal signals in 128-dimensional latent representations at 10-meter resolution globally. It uses self-supervised learning to summarise petabytes of Earth observation data. We compare our work with state-of-the-art task-specific models and other foundation models in five diverse downstream tasks and find that TESSERA closely matches or outperforms these baselines. By preserving temporal phenological signals that are typically lost in conventional approaches, TESSERA enables new insights into ecosystem dynamics, agricultural food systems, and environmental change detection. Moreover, our open-source implementation supports reproducibility and extensibility, while the privacy-preserving design allows researchers to maintain data sovereignty.

originContent: To our knowledge, TESSERA is unprecedented in its ease of use, scale, and accuracy: no other foundation model provides analysis-ready outputs, is open, and provides global, annual coverage at 10m resolution using only spectral-temporal features at pixel level.
translatedContent: To our knowledge, TESSERA is unprecedented in its ease of use, scale, and accuracy: no other foundation model provides analysis-ready outputs, is open, and provides global, annual coverage at 10m resolution using only spectral-temporal features at pixel level.

originContent: If you would like to use our software to create your own embeddings, please follow the instructions below. Note that this is a comptuationally challenging task and you will need access to significant computational and storage resources.
translatedContent: If you would like to use our software to create your own embeddings, please follow the instructions below. Note that this is a comptuationally challenging task and you will need access to significant computational and storage resources.

originContent: Running this pipeline requires substantial storage space. Although the pipeline cleans up some intermediate files after processing, the downloaded raw Sentinel-2 and Sentinel-1 files will still occupy considerable disk space. For example, processing a 100km×100km area from 2022 to output a TESSERA Representation map (10m resolution) requires at least 1TB of storage.
translatedContent: Running this pipeline requires substantial storage space. Although the pipeline cleans up some intermediate files after processing, the downloaded raw Sentinel-2 and Sentinel-1 files will still occupy considerable disk space. For example, processing a 100km×100km area from 2022 to output a TESSERA Representation map (10m resolution) requires at least 1TB of storage.

originContent: We use preprocessed data, initially from Microsoft Planetary Computer. However, the next generation of embeddings will use OPERA from ASF DAAC. In either case, most of the geo-preprocessing has been done. Still, we recommend having at least 128GB of RAM.
translatedContent: We use preprocessed data, initially from Microsoft Planetary Computer. However, the next generation of embeddings will use OPERA from ASF DAAC. In either case, most of the geo-preprocessing has been done. Still, we recommend having at least 128GB of RAM.

originContent: The pipeline has no strict requirements for CPU and GPU, but more CPU cores and more powerful GPUs can significantly speed up inference. When processing a 110km×110km area from 2022, our tests using a 128-core CPU and a single NVIDIA A30 GPU for inference (CPU and GPU each handling 50% of the inference) took approximately 10 hours to complete.
translatedContent: The pipeline has no strict requirements for CPU and GPU, but more CPU cores and more powerful GPUs can significantly speed up inference. When processing a 110km×110km area from 2022, our tests using a 128-core CPU and a single NVIDIA A30 GPU for inference (CPU and GPU each handling 50% of the inference) took approximately 10 hours to complete.

originContent: For the data preprocessing pipeline, we support almost all Linux systems. For Windows, we recommend using WSL. We do not support MacOS at this point.
translatedContent: For the data preprocessing pipeline, we support almost all Linux systems. For Windows, we recommend using WSL. We do not support MacOS at this point.

originContent: For the model inference part, we have only tested it on Linux and Windows WSL, and they are working.
translatedContent: For the model inference part, we have only tested it on Linux and Windows WSL, and they are working.

originContent: _**We strongly recommend that you quickly review the entire tutorial before running the pipeline.**_
translatedContent: _**We strongly recommend that you quickly review the entire tutorial before running the pipeline.**_

originContent: In this step, we stack a full year of Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data along the time dimension to generate a composite. For Sentinel-2, the composite shape is (T,H,W,B), where T is the number of valid observations in that year, and B is the number of bands (we selected 10 bands). For Sentinel-1, we extracted both ascending and descending orbit data. Taking the ascending orbit as an example, the composite shape is (T',H,W,B'), where T' is the number of valid ascending observations in that year, and B' is 2 because we only obtain VV and VH bands.
translatedContent: In this step, we stack a full year of Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data along the time dimension to generate a composite. For Sentinel-2, the composite shape is (T,H,W,B), where T is the number of valid observations in that year, and B is the number of bands (we selected 10 bands). For Sentinel-1, we extracted both ascending and descending orbit data. Taking the ascending orbit as an example, the composite shape is (T',H,W,B'), where T' is the number of valid ascending observations in that year, and B' is 2 because we only obtain VV and VH bands.

originContent: We initially sourced Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data from Microsoft's Planetary Computer:
translatedContent: We initially sourced Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data from Microsoft's Planetary Computer:

originContent: The new generation of embeddings will use OPERA from ASF DAAC:
translatedContent: The new generation of embeddings will use OPERA from ASF DAAC:

originContent: Currently, our pipeline only accepts TIFF format input. The resolution of the input ROI TIFF can vary (e.g., 30m), but the pipeline will **always generate Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 outputs at the configured `RESOLUTION`** (default 10m) while keeping the **ROI extent/bounds identical**. For valid ROI areas within the TIFF, the value is 1; otherwise, it's 0. If you only have a shapefile, that's fine too - we provide a `convert_shp_to_tiff.py` script.
translatedContent: Currently, our pipeline only accepts TIFF format input. The resolution of the input ROI TIFF can vary (e.g., 30m), but the pipeline will **always generate Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 outputs at the configured `RESOLUTION`** (default 10m) while keeping the **ROI extent/bounds identical**. For valid ROI areas within the TIFF, the value is 1; otherwise, it's 0. If you only have a shapefile, that's fine too - we provide a `convert_shp_to_tiff.py` script.

originContent: First, create an empty working directory:
translatedContent: First, create an empty working directory:
For easier pipeline operation, we recommend placing the data output directory at the same level as `tessera_infer` and `tessera_preprocessing`:

originContent: For easier pipeline operation, we recommend placing the data output directory at the same level as `tessera_infer` and `tessera_preprocessing`:
┗ roi.tiff (we recommend generating this using convert_shp_to_tiff.py)
translatedContent: ┗ roi.tiff (we recommend generating this using convert_shp_to_tiff.py)

originContent: The `roi.tiff` can be generated using `convert_shp_to_tiff.py` located in `tessera_preprocessing/convert_shp_to_tiff.py`. To use it, simply specify the path to your shapefile in the main function, and it will output a TIFF with the same name in the same directory.
translatedContent: The `roi.tiff` can be generated using `convert_shp_to_tiff.py` located in `tessera_preprocessing/convert_shp_to_tiff.py`. To use it, simply specify the path to your shapefile in the main function, and it will output a TIFF with the same name in the same directory.

originContent: ⚠️Notice: _If your ROI is relatively large, for example 100 km × 100 km, we strongly recommend pre-splitting the TIFF into smaller sections no larger than 20 km × 20 km. Then process each small TIFF file sequentially in the pipeline. An excessively large ROI may cause issues with backend tile providers_
translatedContent: ⚠️Notice: _If your ROI is relatively large, for example 100 km × 100 km, we strongly recommend pre-splitting the TIFF into smaller sections no larger than 20 km × 20 km. Then process each small TIFF file sequentially in the pipeline. An excessively large ROI may cause issues with backend tile providers_

originContent: We need some geographic processing packages (fortunately, we won't be using GDAL, as configuring the environment is a nightmare) and some machine learning packages (PyTorch, but you'll need to install this yourself since the hardware on each computer is different). We've put some common packages in `requirements.txt`, which you can install as follows:
translatedContent: We need some geographic processing packages (fortunately, we won't be using GDAL, as configuring the environment is a nightmare) and some machine learning packages (PyTorch, but you'll need to install this yourself since the hardware on each computer is different). We've put some common packages in `requirements.txt`, which you can install as follows:

originContent: Note: If you are in a managed environment, you may need to install a venv first, using
translatedContent: Note: If you are in a managed environment, you may need to install a venv first, using

originContent: Then edit the file s1_s2_downloader.sh to point to the ROI TIFF file, the output and temporary directories, and the data source:
translatedContent: Then edit the file s1_s2_downloader.sh to point to the ROI TIFF file, the output and temporary directories, and the data source:

originContent: RESOLUTION=10.0  # Output resolution (meters). ROI TIFF can be any resolution; extent is preserved.
translatedContent: RESOLUTION=10.0  # Output resolution (meters). ROI TIFF can be any resolution; extent is preserved.

originContent: # aws: AWS Open Data backends (S1=OPERA RTC-S1 via ASF/CMR + ASF Earthdata Cloud COGs, S2=Earth-search Sentinel-2 L2A COGs)
translatedContent: # aws: AWS Open Data backends (S1=OPERA RTC-S1 via ASF/CMR + ASF Earthdata Cloud COGs, S2=Earth-search Sentinel-2 L2A COGs)

originContent: Note: `RESOLUTION` controls output pixel size. The pipeline keeps the ROI bounds fixed and resamples the ROI mask into the output grid.
translatedContent: Note: `RESOLUTION` controls output pixel size. The pipeline keeps the ROI bounds fixed and resamples the ROI mask into the output grid.

originContent: Sentinel-2 on Earth-search is public and **does not require credentials**.
translatedContent: Sentinel-2 on Earth-search is public and **does not require credentials**.

originContent: Sentinel-1 OPERA RTC-S1 is accessed via ASF Earthdata Cloud (COG over HTTPS). You need an Earthdata Login token:
translatedContent: Sentinel-1 OPERA RTC-S1 is accessed via ASF Earthdata Cloud (COG over HTTPS). You need an Earthdata Login token:

originContent: - **Approve Application**: After registering your account, you can go to the Applications tab and add Alaska Satellite Facility Data Access to the list of approved applications.
translatedContent: - **Approve Application**: After registering your account, you can go to the Applications tab and add Alaska Satellite Facility Data Access to the list of approved applications.

originContent: - **Obtain an EDL Bearer token / JWT** by clicking **Generate Token** and store it locally (do not commit it).
translatedContent: - **Obtain an EDL Bearer token / JWT** by clicking **Generate Token** and store it locally (do not commit it).

originContent: # paste token, save+exit (Ctrl-O Enter, then Ctrl-X)
translatedContent: # paste token, save+exit (Ctrl-O Enter, then Ctrl-X)

originContent: The AWS S1 downloader will use this token to read COGs from ASF Earthdata Cloud.
translatedContent: The AWS S1 downloader will use this token to read COGs from ASF Earthdata Cloud.

originContent: If you want to retrieve temporary S3 credentials (advanced; usually not required for this pipeline), see ASF guidance:
translatedContent: If you want to retrieve temporary S3 credentials (advanced; usually not required for this pipeline), see ASF guidance:

originContent: Below the above configuration, there are some additional configurations that you can modify according to your computer's performance.
translatedContent: Below the above configuration, there are some additional configurations that you can modify according to your computer's performance.

Due to network conditions, processing some tiles may time out. Our script includes sophisticated timeout management to avoid these issues. However, sometimes some tiles may still fail. Running the above command again usually resolves this.

originContent: Due to network conditions, processing some tiles may time out. Our script includes sophisticated timeout management to avoid these issues. However, sometimes some tiles may still fail. Running the above command again usually resolves this.

translatedContent: If all Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data are generated correctly, they can be stacked along the time dimension. For this step, we use two Rust-generated executables, making it very fast. You can open `s1_s2_stacker.sh` and edit the following:

originContent: If all Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data are generated correctly, they can be stacked along the time dimension. For this step, we use two Rust-generated executables, making it very fast. You can open `s1_s2_stacker.sh` and edit the following:

Normally, we don't modify `DOWNSAMPLE_RATE`, which keeps it from performing any downsampling during stacking. The `BASE_DIR` in the above snippet is the same as the `OUT_DIR` you modified in `s1_s2_downloader.sh`.
translatedContent: Normally, we don't modify `DOWNSAMPLE_RATE`, which keeps it from performing any downsampling during stacking. The `BASE_DIR` in the above snippet is the same as the `OUT_DIR` you modified in `s1_s2_downloader.sh`.

originContent: Then you can execute the stacking:
translatedContent: 然后您可以执行堆叠操作：

originContent: After success, you will get some `.npy` files in `/absolute/path/to/your/data_dir/data_processed`. Usually, these `.npy` files are quite large, so we will patchify them into smaller, more manageable units.
translatedContent: After success, you will get some `.npy` files in `/absolute/path/to/your/data_dir/data_processed`. Usually, these `.npy` files are quite large, so we will patchify them into smaller, more manageable units.

originContent: You can change the above `patch_size` and `block_size` yourself. The above configuration is a recommended configuration for a TIFF with a shape of (5000,5000) and a 10m resolution.
translatedContent: You can change the above `patch_size` and `block_size` yourself. The above configuration is a recommended configuration for a TIFF with a shape of (5000,5000) and a 10m resolution.

originContent: If the above code runs smoothly, you can get some subfolders in `my_data/retiled_d_pixel`.
translatedContent: If the above code runs smoothly, you can get some subfolders in `my_data/retiled_d_pixel`.

Each subfolder should contain the following files:

originContent: Each subfolder should contain the following files:
If these files exist, you can start inference. Otherwise, check if the first step completed successfully.
translatedContent: If these files exist, you can start inference. Otherwise, check if the first step completed successfully.

originContent: Inference requires PyTorch. Since each system may have slightly different CUDA versions, we can't provide a Docker-encapsulated Python environment like we did for data preprocessing. Fortunately, the Python environment for inference is much simpler to configure than for data preprocessing, as it doesn't use geographic processing packages like GDAL or SNAP.
translatedContent: Inference requires PyTorch. Since each system may have slightly different CUDA versions, we can't provide a Docker-encapsulated Python environment like we did for data preprocessing. Fortunately, the Python environment for inference is much simpler to configure than for data preprocessing, as it doesn't use geographic processing packages like GDAL or SNAP.

originContent: If you haven't installed Pytorch, you can refer to the steps below. Otherwise, you can ignore this section.
translatedContent: If you haven't installed Pytorch, you can refer to the steps below. Otherwise, you can ignore this section.

originContent: First, check your system's CUDA version:
translatedContent: First, check your system's CUDA version:
Then visit https://pytorch.org/ and select the appropriate version to install based on your CUDA version, for example:

originContent: Then visit https://pytorch.org/ and select the appropriate version to install based on your CUDA version, for example:
pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128
translatedContent: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128

originContent: Next, download the model weights from [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18RPptbUkCIgUfw1aMdMeOrFML_ZVMszn?usp=sharing) and place the `.pt` file in the `tessera_infer/checkpoints` directory:
translatedContent: Next, download the model weights from [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18RPptbUkCIgUfw1aMdMeOrFML_ZVMszn?usp=sharing) and place the `.pt` file in the `tessera_infer/checkpoints` directory:

originContent: _**Note that the checkpoint mentioned above is an early-stage model, which natively generates float32 embeddings. Therefore, this model is not the one used to generate the int8 embeddings in the geotessera library. We will soon deploy the specific checkpoint that was used to create the geotessera embeddings into the full pipeline.**_
translatedContent: _**Note that the checkpoint mentioned above is an early-stage model, which natively generates float32 embeddings. Therefore, this model is not the one used to generate the int8 embeddings in the geotessera library. We will soon deploy the specific checkpoint that was used to create the geotessera embeddings into the full pipeline.**_

originContent: To simplify inference configuration, we provide `tessera_infer/infer_all_tiles.sh`. You only need to edit a few parameters:
translatedContent: To simplify inference configuration, we provide `tessera_infer/infer_all_tiles.sh`. You only need to edit a few parameters:

originContent: This is your data storage folder, the same as `BASE_DATA_DIR` in the previous bash, e.g., `/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data`
translatedContent: This is your data storage folder, the same as `BASE_DATA_DIR` in the previous bash, e.g., `/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data`

originContent: Write the absolute path to your Python environment here, e.g., `/home/user/anaconda3/envs/tessera_env/bin/python`
translatedContent: Write the absolute path to your Python environment here, e.g., `/home/user/anaconda3/envs/tessera_env/bin/python`

originContent: The script supports simultaneous inference using both CPU and GPU. This ratio specifies the proportion of `retiled_patches` each device will handle. Default is 1:1 (even split). For GPU-only inference, set to 0:1.
translatedContent: The script supports simultaneous inference using both CPU and GPU. This ratio specifies the proportion of `retiled_patches` each device will handle. Default is 1:1 (even split). For GPU-only inference, set to 0:1.

originContent: Maximum number of CPU processes for tile inference. For example, if set to 20, it will process 20 tiles simultaneously.
translatedContent: Maximum number of CPU processes for tile inference. For example, if set to 20, it will process 20 tiles simultaneously.

originContent: Number of CPU cores to use. Please modify this value if necessary to avoid consuming too many CPU resources!
translatedContent: Number of CPU cores to use. Please modify this value if necessary to avoid consuming too many CPU resources!

originContent: Maximum number of GPU processes for inference. If the system has only 1 GPU, set this to 1.
translatedContent: Maximum number of GPU processes for inference. If the system has only 1 GPU, set this to 1.

originContent: GPU_BATCH_SIZE=1024  # Larger for GPU, if this takes too much memory, reduce it
translatedContent: GPU_BATCH_SIZE=1024  # Larger for GPU, if this takes too much memory, reduce it

originContent: Number of samples to process at once during PyTorch inference. If this value consumes too much GPU memory or causes an OOM error on the GPU, please reduce it accordingly.
translatedContent: Number of samples to process at once during PyTorch inference. If this value consumes too much GPU memory or causes an OOM error on the GPU, please reduce it accordingly.

originContent: There are other parameters available for configuration. Please adjust them as needed.
translatedContent: There are other parameters available for configuration. Please adjust them as needed.

originContent: Once everything is ready, navigate to the `tessera_infer` folder:
translatedContent: Once everything is ready, navigate to the `tessera_infer` folder:
If successful, you should see logs like:

originContent: If successful, you should see logs like:
At the same time, a `logs` folder will be generated in the `tessera_infer` folder with more detailed logging for each CPU and GPU process.
translatedContent: At the same time, a `logs` folder will be generated in the `tessera_infer` folder with more detailed logging for each CPU and GPU process.

originContent: Inference usually takes a long time, depending on your ROI size and hardware performance. Once completed, you can find many `.npy` files in `my_data/representation_retiled`:
translatedContent: Inference usually takes a long time, depending on your ROI size and hardware performance. Once completed, you can find many `.npy` files in `my_data/representation_retiled`:

originContent: The final step is to stitch them together using `tessera_infer/stitch_tiled_representation.py`:
translatedContent: The final step is to stitch them together using `tessera_infer/stitch_tiled_representation.py`:

originContent: Finally, you'll get a stitched representation map in the `my_data` directory with the shape (H,W,128), where H and W match your initial `roi.tiff`. The representation map is a NumPy array. If you want to convert it to TIFF for viewing in software like QGIS, you can use the `tessera_infer/convert_npy2tiff.py` script. Just modify the main function with:
translatedContent: Finally, you'll get a stitched representation map in the `my_data` directory with the shape (H,W,128), where H and W match your initial `roi.tiff`. The representation map is a NumPy array. If you want to convert it to TIFF for viewing in software like QGIS, you can use the `tessera_infer/convert_npy2tiff.py` script. Just modify the main function with:

originContent: npy_path = "/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data/stitched_representation.npy"  # Change to the actual npy file path
translatedContent: npy_path = "/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data/stitched_representation.npy"  # Change to the actual npy file path

originContent: ref_tiff_path = "/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data/roi.tiff"  # Change to the actual reference tiff file path
translatedContent: ref_tiff_path = "/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data/roi.tiff"  # Change to the actual reference tiff file path

originContent: out_dir = "/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data/"  # Change to the actual output directory
translatedContent: out_dir = "/maps/usr/tessera_project/my_data/"  # Change to the actual output directory

originContent: If you want to reproduce the downstream tasks in the paper, you can visit https://github.com/ucam-eo/tessera-downstream-task. There are many examples provided there.
translatedContent: If you want to reproduce the downstream tasks in the paper, you can visit https://github.com/ucam-eo/tessera-downstream-task. There are many examples provided there.

translatedContent: * Kenzy Soror (U. Waterloo, working with Robin Young)

originContent: Please direct your technical questions to Frank Feng (zf281@cam.ac.uk) or ask it on our [Zulip forum](https://eeg.zulipchat.com/login/). Non-technical questions can be sent to Prof. S. Keshav (sk818@cam.ac.uk).
translatedContent: Please direct your technical questions to Frank Feng (zf281@cam.ac.uk) or ask it on our [Zulip forum](https://eeg.zulipchat.com/login/). Non-technical questions can be sent to Prof. S. Keshav (sk818@cam.ac.uk).

originContent: If you use TESSERA in your research, please cite the [arXiv paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.20380):
translatedContent: If you use TESSERA in your research, please cite the [arXiv paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.20380):

originContent: title={TESSERA: Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis},
translatedContent: title={TESSERA: Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis},

originContent: We would like to express our gratitude to UKRI and the [DAWN](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/d-w-n) supercomputer team at Cambridge, for their generous support in this project. We also acknowledge support from [AMD](https://www.amd.com/en.html),  [Vultr](https://www.vultr.com/), the [Dirac High Performance Computing Facility](https://dirac.ac.uk), [Microsoft AI For Good Lab](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/group/ai-for-good-research-lab/), Dr. Robert Sansom, [dClimate](https://www.dclimate.net/), and [Amazon Web Services (AWS)](https://aws.amazon.com/) under their AWS Open Data program (https://opendata.aws/). This work would not have been possible without their support, computational resources and technical assistance.
translatedContent: We would like to express our gratitude to UKRI and the [DAWN](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/d-w-n) supercomputer team at Cambridge, for their generous support in this project. We also acknowledge support from [AMD](https://www.amd.com/en.html),  [Vultr](https://www.vultr.com/), the [Dirac High Performance Computing Facility](https://dirac.ac.uk), [Microsoft AI For Good Lab](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/group/ai-for-good-research-lab/), Dr. Robert Sansom, [dClimate](https://www.dclimate.net/), and [Amazon Web Services (AWS)](https://aws.amazon.com/) under their AWS Open Data program (https://opendata.aws/). This work would not have been possible without their support, computational resources and technical assistance.

originContent: ### TESSERA Terms of Use and Ethical Guidelines
translatedContent: ### TESSERA Terms of Use and Ethical Guidelines

originContent: TESSERA data and embeddings are made available under the **Creative Commons 0 International License [CC-0](https://creativecommons.org/public-domain/cc0/)**.
translatedContent: TESSERA data and embeddings are made available under the **Creative Commons 0 International License [CC-0](https://creativecommons.org/public-domain/cc0/)**.

originContent: This means you are free to:
translatedContent: This means you are free to:

originContent: * **Share** — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format
translatedContent: * **Share** — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format

originContent: * **Adapt** — remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially
translatedContent: * **Adapt** — remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially

originContent: TESSERA was developed to advance scientific research and support environmental monitoring, conservation, sustainable agriculture, and understanding of Earth systems. We designed this tool to enable:
translatedContent: TESSERA was developed to advance scientific research and support environmental monitoring, conservation, sustainable agriculture, and understanding of Earth systems. We designed this tool to enable:

originContent: * Climate change research and adaptation planning
translatedContent: * Climate change research and adaptation planning

originContent: * Sustainable land use and resource management
translatedContent: * Sustainable land use and resource management

originContent: * Public interest applications that benefit society and the environment
translatedContent: * Public interest applications that benefit society and the environment

originContent: While the CC0 license permits broad use, we strongly encourage users to consider the ethical implications of their work. These ethical guidelines are advisory and do not impose legally enforceable restrictions. We request that users:
translatedContent: While the CC0 license permits broad use, we strongly encourage users to consider the ethical implications of their work. These ethical guidelines are advisory and do not impose legally enforceable restrictions. We request that users:

originContent: * Consider privacy implications when analyzing specific locations
translatedContent: * Consider privacy implications when analyzing specific locations

originContent: * Respect the rights and dignity of affected communities
translatedContent: * Respect the rights and dignity of affected communities

originContent: * Be mindful of potential dual-use concerns
translatedContent: * Be mindful of potential dual-use concerns

originContent: * Accurately represent the data's characteristics (annual resolution, 10m spatial resolution)
translatedContent: * Accurately represent the data's characteristics (annual resolution, 10m spatial resolution)

originContent: * Consider how your work contributes to societal benefit
translatedContent: * Consider how your work contributes to societal benefit

originContent: * Engage with affected communities when appropriate
translatedContent: * Engage with affected communities when appropriate

originContent: * Share findings that advance public knowledge
translatedContent: * Share findings that advance public knowledge

originContent: Users should understand that TESSERA provides:
translatedContent: Users should understand that TESSERA provides:

originContent: * **Annual temporal resolution** — data represents yearly summaries, not real-time or high-frequency monitoring
translatedContent: * **Annual temporal resolution** — data represents yearly summaries, not real-time or high-frequency monitoring

originContent: * **10-meter spatial resolution** — suitable for landscape-scale analysis
translatedContent: * **10-meter spatial resolution** — suitable for landscape-scale analysis

originContent: * **Spectral-temporal embeddings** — compressed representations, not raw imagery
translatedContent: * **Spectral-temporal embeddings** — compressed representations, not raw imagery

originContent: Please accurately represent these characteristics in your work.
translatedContent: Please accurately represent these characteristics in your work.

originContent: We encourage responsible use and welcome community feedback. If you have concerns about potential applications or suggestions for improving these guidelines, please contact us.
translatedContent: We encourage responsible use and welcome community feedback. If you have concerns about potential applications or suggestions for improving these guidelines, please contact us.

originContent: We reserve the right to update these guidelines based on community input and emerging considerations, though such updates do not retroactively affect the CC-0 license under which data is released.
translatedContent: We reserve the right to update these guidelines based on community input and emerging considerations, though such updates do not retroactively affect the CC-0 license under which data is released.

originContent: For questions or feedback: Email sk818@cam.ac.uk
translatedContent: For questions or feedback: Email sk818@cam.ac.uk
